sources.role: deprecations + Vertex batch tiers + policy flags

money: integer micro-USD; token-metered rows use the existing per-Mtok fields, media rows use unit_price_micros per pricing_unit; null = unknown/not offered
note: Direct first-party authoritative layer. Complements config/model-registry.json (OpenRouter-derived). On conflict, this layer's official-page citations win. Cross-check `flags` rows before treating any price as billing-grade. See docs/registry/RECONCILIATION.md for cross-source agreement + discrepancies.

routing_policy_flags.allow_batch: Vertex Flex/Batch (e.g. gemini-2.5-pro 0.625/5.00 vs 1.25/10.00 standard) — batch semantics only
routing_policy_flags.allow_promo: time-boxed promo pricing (e.g. deepseek-v4-pro until 2026-05-31 15:59 UTC) — expires
routing_policy_flags.allow_aggregator: third-party hosts of open weights (Together/Fireworks/Novita/...) vs first-party
routing_policy_flags.allow_free: free tiers / free routes (Groq, Cerebras, OpenRouter :free, NVIDIA Build free endpoints, Z.ai Flash) — non-SLA only

providers.name: ComfyUI (local)
providers.rate_limit_note: On-demand local executor on a normally-powered-off batch machine; switchback wakes it, drains the queue, and powers it off after an idle timeout. Endpoint and wake/poweroff commands are operator config (neutral placeholder shown).

providers.name: vLLM (local, OpenAI-compatible)
providers.rate_limit_note: On-demand local OpenAI-compatible text executor on a normally-powered-off batch machine; managed by the same capacity activator as comfy-local. Endpoint and wake/poweroff commands are operator config (neutral placeholder shown).

routing_notes: Good scout lane for fast open-weight text and agentic systems; verify per-model limits from the models page before production routing.
Do not treat temperature=0 as exact determinism; Groq documents conversion to a tiny nonzero value.

provider_sources.note: OpenAI compatibility, base URL, unsupported parameters, Responses API.
Model IDs, pricing, rate limits, context and output limits.
Tool-use surface uses JSON-schema tool definitions.

routing_notes: Broad open-model host with serverless and dedicated endpoints; batch discounts are a native Together path, not OpenAI-shaped /v1 batches.
Seed support is best-effort only, so deterministic eval lanes need Switchback probes.

provider_sources.note: OpenAI-compatible endpoints, base URL, capability matrix and seed caveat.
Serverless catalog, model categories, pricing and rate-limit notes.

routing_notes: Strong host for open-source text models with both serverless and dedicated deployments.
Structured output support is explicit, but deterministic eval routes still need probes.

provider_sources.note: OpenAI-compatible text model API and base URL.
OpenAI-compatible function/tool calling.
JSON schema and grammar structured outputs.

routing_notes: Useful as a price cross-check host because pricing pages expose per-model context, cached-input prices, and current token prices.
Priority tier can add surcharge; score routing should separate standard and priority economics.

provider_sources.note: OpenAI-compatible chat completions, base URL and supported parameter surface.
Per-model pricing, context windows and cached-input prices.

routing_notes: Official 2026 docs use https://api.novita.ai/openai for OpenAI-compatible LLM APIs; the older /v3/openai URL is retained only as provenance.
List Models returns pricing and context size, so this provider is a good candidate for an auth-backed catalog adapter.

provider_sources.note: Official base URLs and LLM API endpoint groups.
Authenticated OpenAI-compatible model list with prices and context sizes.
Function calling guide and supported-model framing.

routing_notes: Public endpoint models are documented as free subject to rate limits; use as fast/free scout, not final certifier without probes.
Public catalog exposes supported parameters, pricing, capabilities and architecture without an API key.

provider_sources.note: OpenAI compatibility and base URL.
Public model catalog and free public endpoint note.
No-auth public model API with pricing, capabilities and supported parameters.

routing_notes: SambaCloud catalog is small and explicit; free-tier rate limits include request/day and token/day concepts.
Use as independent host cross-check for MiniMax, DeepSeek, Llama and GPT-OSS model availability.

provider_sources.note: Developer guide and OpenAI compatibility pointers.
SambaCloud model IDs, context lengths and modalities.
Rate-limit dimensions and free-tier token/day note.

routing_notes: Good independent scout host for open models; docs expose basic tier RPM and IP caps that routing can use after adapter support.
Model deprecation list must be watched because docs mark several popular open models for sunset.

provider_sources.note: Serverless inference overview, base URL, tiers, capabilities and deprecation notes.
Chat completions endpoint, streaming and supported request parameters.
OpenAI SDK integration and base URL.

routing_notes: Nebius public docs now point to Token Factory rather than the older AI Studio base URL; registry keeps the previous URL as provenance only.
Treat as an EU-governed independent host candidate for open models after auth-backed catalog import.

provider_sources.note: Token Factory quickstart, base URL and OpenAI-compatible API positioning.
Model families, multimodal/reasoning coverage, function calling and pricing positioning.

routing_notes: Energy pricing is a separate metered cost dimension; token price rows are not enough to estimate real request cost.
Energy averages are rolling observed values from live traffic, so refresh before promoting routes that optimize for energy cost.

provider_sources.note: OpenAI-compatible base URL and chat completions examples.
Token prices, cached-input prices, context windows and feature badges.
Trailing observed energy averages by model and prompt-size band.

providers.name: fal.ai

provider_sources.note: Seed values supplied by work_3028b7e7d47c; re-verify via refresh.

providers.rate_limit_note: verified 2026-07-15: fal gates on concurrent in-flight requests, NOT rpm — new accounts 2 concurrent, auto-scales with paid credit volume to self-serve max 40, above 40 = sales; queued requests do not count; 429 type=concurrent_requests_limit with X-Fal-needs-retry:1. Parallel fan-out must respect account concurrency.
providers.balance_api_note: verified 2026-07-15: GET https://api.fal.ai/v1/account/billing?expand=credits requires an ADMIN api key (not any key); response {username, credits:{current_balance,currency}}

models.name: ComfyUI local workflow
models.note: Local executor; 0 = known-free (own hardware/electricity). null would mean unknown.

models.name: vLLM local text model

models.notes: DEPRECATED on fal per live page 2026-07-15 — endpoint marked no longer supported; 400000/s is the AUDIO-ON rate (audio-off 200000/s); successor fal-ai/veo3.1 (active) — re-point + re-verify price before routing to it

spread.note: Tight band; Groq adds cache-hit discount + speed.
~9x input spread for identical weights — host choice dominates.
Direct API ~4-5x cheaper than aggregator markup (promo).
Direct and Novita tie on headline; DeepSeek has 10x cheaper cache.
Z.ai direct ties with Bedrock on headline token price.
Direct Mistral ties with Bedrock; both 0.50/1.50 (Oracle x2).
Direct cheaper on input; aggregators add convenience.
Aggregator can undercut the first party.

metadata_contract.status: declared provider facts + optional Switchback probe receipts
metadata_contract.money: integer micro-USD per 1M tokens (USD/Mtok * 1e6); null = not offered / priced-per-host

fields.determinism: Declared knobs such as seed; deterministic behavior still needs probe receipt.
fields.architecture: Dense/MoE/parameter facts with source; absent means unknown, not dense.
fields.benchmarks: Vendor or third-party benchmark values, never treated as local certification.
fields.verification: Switchback probe receipts go here; declared facts alone are not proof.
fields.freshness: Catalog, provenance, and probe timestamps are evidence freshness; model availability, prices, and capabilities can change.
fields.provider_research: Provider-level official docs cross-checks for hosts that do not yet have ingested model rows.

notes: Good scout lane for fast open-weight text and agentic systems; verify per-model limits from the models page before production routing.
Do not treat temperature=0 as exact determinism; Groq documents conversion to a tiny nonzero value.
Broad open-model host with serverless and dedicated endpoints; batch discounts are a native Together path, not OpenAI-shaped /v1 batches.
Seed support is best-effort only, so deterministic eval lanes need Switchback probes.
Strong host for open-source text models with both serverless and dedicated deployments.
Structured output support is explicit, but deterministic eval routes still need probes.
Useful as a price cross-check host because pricing pages expose per-model context, cached-input prices, and current token prices.
Priority tier can add surcharge; score routing should separate standard and priority economics.
Official 2026 docs use https://api.novita.ai/openai for OpenAI-compatible LLM APIs; the older /v3/openai URL is retained only as provenance.
List Models returns pricing and context size, so this provider is a good candidate for an auth-backed catalog adapter.
Public endpoint models are documented as free subject to rate limits; use as fast/free scout, not final certifier without probes.
Public catalog exposes supported parameters, pricing, capabilities and architecture without an API key.
SambaCloud catalog is small and explicit; free-tier rate limits include request/day and token/day concepts.
Use as independent host cross-check for MiniMax, DeepSeek, Llama and GPT-OSS model availability.
Good independent scout host for open models; docs expose basic tier RPM and IP caps that routing can use after adapter support.
Model deprecation list must be watched because docs mark several popular open models for sunset.
Nebius public docs now point to Token Factory rather than the older AI Studio base URL; registry keeps the previous URL as provenance only.
Treat as an EU-governed independent host candidate for open models after auth-backed catalog import.
Energy pricing is a separate metered cost dimension; token price rows are not enough to estimate real request cost.
Energy averages are rolling observed values from live traffic, so refresh before promoting routes that optimize for energy cost.
